RED: T612-escape (scripts/test-adaptive-node.js) FAILS against the pre-fix (fail-open) writeRunProgressMirror.
Fixture: a real linked worktree whose project (issue-612e) exists ONLY in the worktree; mainRoot resolves via
the git-common-dir fallback (no main_root: field) to a checkout that does NOT own the project. open-next (a
LEDGER_MUTATING op) fires the mirror. Observed:
  FAIL: T612-escape: the mirror must NOT be written to a heuristic-resolved main that does not own the project
  (fail-closed), but it appeared at .../kw-612-esc-main-XXXX/kaola-workflow/issue-612e/.cache/run-progress.json
  adaptive-node tests FAILED (1 failures, 1398 passed)
This is the #612 leak class reproduced deterministically: the fail-open mirror fabricates a foreign
kaola-workflow/<project>/ tree on an untrusted (heuristic-resolved) mainRoot.
<!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: After the fail-closed fix (writeRunProgressMirror now writes ONLY when mainRoot is trusted — resolved
from the project's own workflow-state.md main_root: field, OR a fallback root that DEMONSTRABLY already owns the
project via a present workflow-plan.md), the same suite passes:
  adaptive-node tests passed (1399 assertions)
T612-escape now GREEN (mirror skipped on the foreign root, no warn field), T612-legit GREEN (mirror STILL written
when main owns the project — legitimate #605 path unchanged). Fix applied identically to all 4 node.js editions
(scripts/ + 3 plugins); kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js untouched. Cross-edition drift green
(validate-script-sync.js OK; edition-sync.js --check OK). The real #605(a)+(b) walkthrough mirror fixtures
verified GREEN against the patched code via a faithful replica (5/5 asserts): mirror still written when main owns
the project, fail-open-on-unwritable warn preserved.
